automated.summary: Two gate regression tests added for the forge init collision check: a non-empty-but-non-colliding target (pre-seeded git repo with its own doc) scaffolds successfully with the pre-existing file byte-identical, and a target containing a scaffold-owned file (WORKFLOW.md) is refused with exit 1 naming the collision and leaving the file untouched. Full gate suite green via verify.py (structural: check_dual_runtime, scaffold check, tests: pytest .agents/tests). End-to-end reproduction of the original failure also passes: init into a repo holding .git + docs/spec.md succeeds; re-init into the scaffolded dir refuses listing 147 collisions.
commands_run: python3 .agents/scripts/forge.py init --name e2e --target <scratch dir with .git + docs/spec.md>
automated.pass_fail_summary: gate suite green including 2 new tests; e2e scenario passes both directions
